---
title: "Master Thesis Research Questions"
phase: thesis
audience: advisor
status: current
owner: jan
format: html
---

# Master Thesis Research Questions {#research-questions}

This page states the research questions for the master's thesis phase of
**ARIA-NBV**. The central question is:

> Can ARIA-NBV train a target-conditioned NBV scorer and finite-candidate
> fitted $Q_H$ policy that maximizes cumulative
> {{< glsfull target-specific-rri >}} for a
> {{< gls target-of-interest >}} under a fixed acquisition budget?

The questions are tied to the dated [roadmap](roadmap.qmd), the
[RRI theory page](../theory/rri_theory.qmd), the
[RL and bounded rollout theory page](../theory/rl_planning.qmd), the
[oracle/RRI implementation notes](../impl/rri_computation.qmd), the
[VIN implementation notes](../impl/vin_nbv.qmd), and the current ASE/EVL stack
[@ProjectAria-ASE-2025; @EFM3D-straub2024]. The thesis builds on VIN-NBV's
quality-driven candidate-ranking objective [@VIN-NBV-frahm2025], while treating
GenNBV [@GenNBV-chen2024], Hestia [@Hestia-lu2026], and SceneScript
[@SceneScript-avetisyan2024] as comparison and extension references.

## Definitions {#rq-definitions}

The core terms used by the research questions are maintained in the shared
[glossary](../glossary.qmd), which is generated from
`docs/typst/shared/glossary.typ` for Quarto, Typst, and KG ingestion:

- {{< glsfull target-of-interest >}}
- {{< glsfull target-specific-rri >}}
- {{< glsfull acquisition-cost >}}
- {{< glsfull target-conditioned-scorer >}}
- {{< glsfull observed-target-selection >}}
- {{< glsfull predicted-target-q >}}
- {{< glsfull ground-truth-target-evaluation >}}

Related implementation nodes remain [VINv3](../impl/vin_nbv.qmd),
[EVL literature notes](../literature/efm3d.qmd), and
[candidate generation](../../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CandidateViewGenerator.qmd).

## Thesis Boundary {#rq-thesis-boundary}

The thesis should remain explicit about what is already implemented, what must
be completed before scale-up, what is the hard thesis core, and what remains
stretch work. This boundary protects the proposal and final thesis from
presenting ARIA-NBV as a finished continuous-control or real-device RL policy
before the evidence exists.

| Status | Work included |
|---|---|
| Already implemented substrate | Scene-level oracle RRI, immutable VIN offline-store paths, VINv3-style one-step scoring, candidate generation, rollout scaffolding, RolloutTrace smoke storage, and Rerun offline inspection. |
| Prerequisite core | Proposal freeze, M1 contract report, V0/V1 target contracts, candidate-label/frame guards, invalidity masks/reasons, rollout/Q storage schema, and LRZ deterministic sharding/storage gates. |
| Hard thesis core | Observed-only automatic target selector; OBS-SEL / PRED-Q / GT-EVAL protocol; target-conditioned one-step scorer; mixed observed candidate sets; random-valid, greedy, softmax, Gumbel, and bounded oracle-lookahead rollouts; fitted Double-Q / $Q_H$ over finite candidates; full-scale LRZ generation on the 100 GT-mesh ASE scenes and 4,608 snippet windows. |
| Stretch work | IQL as a second offline-RL ablation, actor-critic or continuous-policy bridge design, Habitat/Isaac/external simulators, SceneScript-style semantic memory, VLM/global planning, real-device guidance, and quantitative continuous-control baselines. |

## Proposal Freeze Gate {#rq-proposal-freeze-gate}

Before M1 scale-up, the proposal, roadmap, research questions, and literature
pages must state the same advisor-facing contract:

- ARIA-NBV uses target-specific RRI as the thesis utility signal, with scene
  RRI and acquisition cost reported separately;
- V1 OBS-SEL / PRED-Q / GT-EVAL is mandatory for the main result, while V0 GT
  OBB input is only a sanity or upper-bound path;
- target-conditioned fitted Double-Q / $Q_H$ over finite candidate sets is a
  hard thesis deliverable after the M1/M3/M4 gates, and any blocker must be
  documented explicitly;
- continuous actor-critic policies, online Gymnasium/SB3, Habitat/Isaac,
  3DGS-backed control, SceneScript/global memory, and real-device deployment
  remain bridge or future work unless M5 evidence justifies them;
- proposal-critical citations must resolve through `docs/references.bib` to
  primary paper, dataset, or library metadata rather than generated comments or
  Wikipedia references.

## RQ1 - Objective {#rq1-objective}

**Question.** Can ARIA-NBV learn target-centric, RRI-based multi-step NBV
prediction in complex indoor ASE scenes using finite candidate sets and
target-conditioned state?

**Working hypothesis.** The thesis should optimize cumulative target RRI first
and report acquisition cost or feasibility separately. Additional penalty terms
can change the learned objective and should only be introduced as explicit
scalarized ablations after the quality-only baseline is stable.

**Concrete formulation.** For a target $e$ and trajectory $\tau$:

$$
J_e^{\mathrm{quality}}(\tau) =
\sum_{t=1}^{T} \operatorname{RRI}_e(q_t \mid P_{t-1}, M_e)
$$

where $P_{t-1}$ is the accumulated reconstruction state before selecting
candidate view $q_t$, and $M_e$ is the target-specific ground-truth surface used
only for oracle supervision and evaluation. Acquisition cost and feasibility
terms such as view count, path length, runtime, and invalid-action rate are
reported alongside $J_e^{\mathrm{quality}}$ under equal-budget comparisons. A
scalarized objective $J_e^{\mathrm{quality}}(\tau) - \lambda C(\tau)$ is an
extension, not the first training target.

**Evaluation nodes.**

- target RRI, scene RRI, and cumulative target RRI;
- view count, path length, invalid-action rate, and runtime;
- comparison to one-step greedy selection under equal budget.

**Multi-step reward convention.** Multi-step experiments should start with an
additive cumulative target-RRI reward. Episode normalization or log-improvement
variants are ablations after the additive quality baseline is trusted. The
existing one-step RRI label remains unchanged for VIN compatibility and for
comparison against the one-step greedy baseline.

## RQ2 - Target Encoding {#rq2-target-encoding}

**Question.** Which target representation works best for conditioning the NBV
model and $Q_H$: GT OBB, predicted/observed OBB, crop descriptor, entity token,
or target point?

**Working hypothesis.** Separate privileged labels from actor-visible target
state. V0 starts with GT OBB input plus GT crop/evaluation as a sanity and
upper-bound path. V1 is mandatory for the main result: target selection and
scorer/$Q_H$ inputs use observed or predicted OBBs, class, confidence, relative
geometry, projected area, and current semidense/EVL support, while matched GT
OBBs define target-RRI labels and evaluation.

**Main protocol.** The main thesis protocol is
{{< gls observed-target-selection >}} / {{< gls predicted-target-q >}} /
{{< gls ground-truth-target-evaluation >}}. Observed-only target selection and
predicted/observed target-conditioned scoring are actor-visible; GT target crops
are used for oracle labels and evaluation only. GT OBBs are never
actor-visible in the main result.

**Initial detector.** Actor-visible targets come from the employed
{{< gls egocentric-foundation-model-3d >}} / {{< gls egocentric-voxel-lifting >}}
stack, which exposes predicted or tracked {{< gls oriented-bounding-box >}}
fields, semantic class probabilities, confidence-style scores, and voxel or
semidense support signals.

**Target descriptors.**

- GT OBB: center, extents, orientation, class, confidence placeholder, and
  pose relative to the reference rig; V0 sanity and upper-bound only.
- Predicted/observed OBB: same geometric contract, sourced from EVL/EFM
  predictions or observed support signals; mandatory V1 actor-visible input.
- Crop descriptor: pooled EVL/voxel features or point statistics inside the
  target crop.
- Entity token: learned token for target identity, class, and geometry.
- Target point: look-at point, object center, missing-surface centroid, or
  expected-RRI centroid.

**Encoding functions.** Descriptor fields should be encoded separately from the
choice of descriptor source. Candidate encodings include relative target
translation and extent, R6D-style relative orientation, class-probability or
semantic embeddings, projected-area/support scalars, and learned Fourier
features or MLP encoders for continuous geometry. Crop descriptors can enter
through pooled EVL/voxel features once the OBB-level contract is stable.

**Matching rule.** Observed targets are matched to GT target labels by
compatible class, OBB IoU, visibility/support, projected area, and semidense or
EVL point support. If M3 evidence exposes a systematic mismatch not covered by
those signals, the matching contract should add a named criterion with an
explicit failure mode rather than carrying an unnamed placeholder.

**Connected implementation surfaces.** Target fields should enter through
data-handling containers, then flow into the
[VIN model API](../../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd) and
diagnostics rather than through ad hoc dictionaries.

**First metric.** The first target-aware metric is GT-OBB-cropped target RRI.
The main thesis input path, however, must graduate from V0 GT input to V1
observed/predicted target input before target-conditioned scorer or $Q_H$
claims are made.

## RQ3 - Candidate and Action Space {#rq3-candidates}

**Question.** Do object-centric candidates plus default exploration candidates
outperform generic shell sampling?

**Working hypothesis.** A mixed sampler should outperform a pure generic shell:
object-centric candidates focus on the target, while default candidates preserve
exploration and reduce overfitting to a single view pattern.

**Candidate families.**

- `TARGET_POINT` candidates that look at a predicted/observed target point or
  OBB center.
- `RADIAL_AWAY` and `RADIAL_TOWARDS` shell candidates around the current
  reference pose for default exploration and augmentation.
- `FORWARD_RIG` candidates that preserve trajectory-forward view direction.
- Position sampling with the existing `UNIFORM_SPHERE` and
  `FORWARD_POWERSPHERICAL` strategies where appropriate.
- Existing yaw, pitch, and roll jitter superimposed on the chosen base
  orientation.
- A categorical-probability mixture over the package-supported families above.

Frontier, missing-surface, or expected-RRI heatmap candidates are stretch
families unless they are implemented and validated in the generator.

**Evaluation nodes.** Candidate realism, valid fraction, target visibility,
target RRI distribution, scene RRI distribution, selected-view diversity, and
strategy provenance for every candidate set.

## RQ4 - Target-Conditioned Model {#rq4-model}

**Question.** Can a one-step {{< gls target-conditioned-scorer >}} rank
candidates by {{< gls target-specific-rri >}} better than a scene-level scorer?

**Working hypothesis.** Target conditioning should improve ranking for selected
objects and regions, especially when scene-level RRI would prefer walls, floors,
or large background surfaces.

**Baselines.**

- Scene-level VIN scorer trained on scene RRI.
- Target-conditioned one-step scorer trained on target RRI.
- Oracle target RRI ranking.
- Simple geometric target-visibility heuristic.

**Success criteria.** The target-conditioned scorer should improve target-level
rank correlation, top-k oracle hit rate, and selected-target RRI without causing
unacceptable scene-level regressions. The $Q_H$ result is evaluated in
[RQ5](#rq5-planning): it must beat one-step greedy/model scoring on cumulative
target RRI under equal acquisition budget.

## RQ5 - Multi-Step Planning {#rq5-planning}

**Question.** Do multi-step target-aware rollouts and fitted $Q_H$ selection
beat one-step greedy/model selection under equal acquisition budget?

**Working hypothesis.** Bounded multi-step rollouts should improve cumulative
target RRI when the first best target view is not locally obvious or when a
short path must move around occlusion. Fitted Double-Q / $Q_H$ should learn
enough of this bounded return structure from ASE oracle rollout traces to beat
one-step greedy/model scoring under the same acquisition budget.

**Planning variants.**

- random valid selection;
- one-step greedy oracle and one-step model/VIN scoring;
- bounded oracle-RRI lookahead under the same candidate and view budget as
  one-step greedy, used as an upper bound;
- temperature-softmax rollouts;
- Gumbel-Top-k rollouts with explicit per-step branch schedules;
- target-conditioned fitted Double-Q / $Q_H$ over finite candidate sets.

**First comparison.** The first thesis-grade non-myopic comparison is bounded
oracle-RRI lookahead versus one-step greedy selection under equal acquisition
budget. This isolates whether planning depth helps before claiming that a
learned model or policy improves trajectories.

**Gate ordering.** Deterministic oracle-greedy and bounded oracle-lookahead
rollouts must be trusted before stochastic/Gumbel branching is treated as
training data, and random-valid, greedy, bounded oracle lookahead,
temperature-softmax, and Gumbel traces must be stable before $Q_H$ training.
The $Q_H$ objective predicts bounded cumulative target RRI. Scalar motion,
rule, or validity penalties are later extensions.

**Simulator substrate.** The thesis-core simulator is the ASE mesh/oracle
counterfactual rollout loop. Habitat, Isaac, and online Gymnasium/SB3 are not
the substrate for the main $Q_H$ result.

**Success bar.** Learned $Q_H$ must beat one-step greedy/model scoring on
cumulative target RRI under equal acquisition budget. Bounded oracle lookahead
is the upper bound and random-valid is the lower reference.

### Minimal ArgTopK Rollout Sequence {#rq5-argtopk-rollout-sequence}

The current implementation already has the right abstraction for the next
research step: `CounterfactualPoseGeneratorConfig` exposes an explicit horizon,
branch factor, beam width, and selection policy, while
`CounterfactualOracleRriScorer` can score valid rollout candidates with oracle
RRI on the accumulated counterfactual point state. This means the next
contribution should not begin as continuous control. It should begin as a
finite-horizon selector over typed candidate sets, keeping the VIN-NBV
quality-driven ranking objective intact [@VIN-NBV-frahm2025] while delaying the
continuous 5-DoF policy-learning problem exemplified by GenNBV
[@GenNBV-chen2024].

Let $s_t$ denote the geometry-first rollout state, $C(s_t)$ the valid candidate
set sampled around the current reference pose, $r(s_t,q)$ the oracle RRI
obtained after fusing candidate $q$, and $T(s_t,q)$ the deterministic
counterfactual transition that appends the selected candidate observation to
the accumulated point state. The one-step baseline is
$\operatorname{ArgTop1}_1(s_t)=\arg\max_{q \in C(s_t)} r(s_t,q)$. For a horizon
$h>1$, bounded lookahead should first compute
$\operatorname{ArgTopK}(s_t)$ as the $K$ best valid candidates under the same
one-step score, then recurse only through those branches:

$$
V_h(s_t)=
\max_{q \in \operatorname{ArgTopK}(s_t)}
\left[r(s_t,q)+V_{h-1}(T(s_t,q))\right],
\qquad
\operatorname{ArgTop1}_h(s_t)=
\arg\max_{q \in \operatorname{ArgTopK}(s_t)}
\left[r(s_t,q)+V_{h-1}(T(s_t,q))\right].
$$

The resulting experimental ladder is therefore
$\operatorname{ArgTopK} \rightarrow \operatorname{ArgTop1}_1 \rightarrow
\operatorname{ArgTop1}_2 \rightarrow \cdots \rightarrow
\operatorname{ArgTop1}_H$. In practical terms, `ArgTop1_1` is the trusted
greedy oracle baseline, `ArgTop1_h` is the first action selected by an
$h$-step oracle rollout, and the branch factor $K$ is the computational budget
that keeps the tree inspectable. This progression follows the same decoding
logic that makes beam search useful for offline trajectory models
[@TrajectoryTransformer-janner2021] and can later be made stochastic with
Gumbel-Top-k sampling without replacement [@GumbelTopK-kool2019]. It also
matches Hestia's lesson that
high-dimensional NBV control benefits from structured, close-greedy or
hierarchical decomposition [@Hestia-lu2026], but it keeps ARIA-NBV's first
claim narrower: planning depth is useful only if
$\operatorname{ArgTop1}_h$ improves cumulative RRI over
$\operatorname{ArgTop1}_1$ under the same acquisition budget.

**Actor-visible state.** The first rollout actor state is geometry-first:
accumulated semidense/current points, candidate poses, candidate validity and
diagnostic metadata, observed/predicted target OBB fields, and bounded history.
Oracle labels, GT mesh quantities, and GT OBB crops are supervision and
evaluation signals, not actor-visible inputs in the main V1 protocol.

**Connected implementation surfaces.** Use the
[CounterfactualPoseGenerator](../../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CounterfactualPoseGenerator.qmd)
and keep horizon, branch factor, and beam width explicit so planning complexity
stays bounded.

## RQ6 - Feasibility and Invalid Actions {#rq6-feasibility}

**Question.** How should invalid actions be handled in one-step scoring,
rollout generation, and $Q_H$ training?

**Working hypothesis.** Hard masks and explicit reason codes are mandatory
because they preserve label semantics. A validity head or penalty reward is an
ablation only after the oracle and candidate generator expose invalid reasons
consistently.

**Invalidity is not a low RRI class.** Collision, out-of-bounds poses,
insufficient target support, and missing candidate evidence are different from a
valid candidate with low target improvement. Treating all invalid actions as the
lowest ordinal RRI bin would blur feasibility and reconstruction quality.

**Evaluation nodes.** Invalid fraction, invalid reason distribution,
target-visible fraction, rank metrics with and without masks, and penalty/head
ablation once labels are reliable. $Q_H$ consumes the masks and reason codes;
invalid rows are not trained as the lowest target-RRI return.

**Q evidence gate.** Before $Q_H$ is treated as a thesis result, the one-step
model scorer needs held-out ranking evidence, oracle-evaluated model-selected
rollouts, calibration and stage-shift analysis, and Rerun visualizations of
representative successes and failures. The first value-learning reference is
masked fitted Double-Q-style training for overestimation control
[@DoubleDQN-vanHasselt2015]. IQL [@IQL-kostrikov2021] is a second offline-RL
ablation after $Q_H$ is stable, not a prerequisite for the main result.

## RQ7 - Scaling and Ablations {#rq7-scaling}

**Question.** Does more supervision coverage buy more than architecture changes,
and can the final thesis evidence scale to the full ASE GT-mesh subset?

**Working hypothesis.** Before large model rewrites, scale within the current
mesh-supervised ASE subset: more snippets, more anchor poses, broader candidate
sets, target-aware labels, and rollout/Q_H traces. Architecture changes should
be justified by controlled ablations. Small trusted subsets are for
correctness; final experiments target the full 100 GT-mesh ASE scenes and 4,608
snippet windows.

**Scale axes.**

- more of the 100 GT-mesh ASE scenes and available snippets;
- more anchor poses per trajectory;
- more candidates and broader candidate distributions;
- target-aware labels for multiple objects per snippet;
- rollout and Q_H traces with stable lineage;
- stage-aware calibration and binning analysis.

**Coverage reporting.** Every supervision-scale claim should report the actual
mesh-supervised coverage used: the 100-scene GT-mesh ASE limit, the 4,608
available snippet windows, the current/offline subset size where applicable,
scene-level train/val split, number of anchor poses, number of candidates, and
number of target entities per snippet. The final report should either use all
100 GT-mesh scenes / 4,608 snippets or state the exact missing coverage and why
it remains blocked. Architecture, rollout, or Q_H conclusions should not be
compared across runs that silently change this coverage.

**Storage and cluster gate.** Rollout and Q_H data should use a
[Zarr-first rollout/Q store](../impl/rollout_storage_contract.qmd) that is rich
enough for training and evaluation without duplicating raw ASE/ATEK. Full
meshes are external path/hash/version references; high-detail target mesh crops
are embedded once per target with crop metadata. LRZ deterministic sharding,
Slurm/DSS staging, resume-safe writes, and storage budgeting are hard M2/M3
gates before full-scale generation.

**Split protocol.** Train/validation/test boundaries are scene-level. The
held-out test split remains an advisor decision, but sample-level leakage is
not acceptable for final claims.

**Ablation axes.** Surface reconstruction input, CORAL variant, auxiliary
regression, candidate-relative pose encoding, target encoding, candidate sampler
mixture, and invalid-action handling.

## RQ8 - Extensions and Boundaries {#rq8-extensions}

**Question.** Which ambitious directions are extensions rather than thesis-core
requirements?

**Working boundary.** The thesis core is target-conditioned RRI plus
multi-step rollouts plus fitted $Q_H$ over finite candidate sets generated by
the ASE mesh/oracle counterfactual rollout loop. The following remain stretch
work unless they become necessary and feasible before the roadmap gate:

- IQL as a second offline-RL ablation after $Q_H$ is stable;
- actor-critic or continuous-policy bridge design, without requiring a
  quantitative continuous-control baseline;
- VLM/global planning over grounded portals, frontiers, entities, and regions;
- continuous target-then-pose control inspired by Hestia;
- simulator-backed online RL with Habitat, Isaac, or external datasets;
- SceneScript or open-vocabulary entity memory as a global semantic layer;
- real-device deployment and human-in-the-loop AR guidance.

These extensions should be written as future work unless they receive
experiment-grade evidence before the thesis freeze.

## Research Matrix {#rq-matrix}

| Question | Roadmap gate | Primary evidence | Main linked surface |
|---|---|---|---|
| [RQ1](#rq1-objective) | M1, M5 | target RRI and acquisition-cost curves | [RRI theory](../theory/rri_theory.qmd) |
| [RQ2](#rq2-target-encoding) | M3, M4 | target-encoding ablation | [EVL notes](../literature/efm3d.qmd) |
| [RQ3](#rq3-candidates) | M3, M5 | candidate sampler ablation | [Candidate API](../../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CandidateViewGenerator.qmd) |
| [RQ4](#rq4-model) | M4 | scene vs target scorer ranking metrics | [VIN API](../../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd) |
| [RQ5](#rq5-planning) | M5 | one-step vs multi-step cumulative RRI and Q_H success bar | [Rollout API](../../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CounterfactualPoseGenerator.qmd) |
| [RQ6](#rq6-feasibility) | M3, M5 | invalid-action masks, reason codes, and ablations | [Candidate API](../../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CandidateSamplingResult.qmd) |
| [RQ7](#rq7-scaling) | M2 to M7 | full-scale coverage, Zarr/LRZ reports, and ablation tables | [Data pipeline](../impl/data_pipeline_overview.qmd) |
| [RQ8](#rq8-extensions) | M6 | IQL/actor-critic bridge decision and future-work text | [Roadmap](roadmap.qmd#roadmap-m6) |

## KG-Friendly Question Writing {#rq-kg-writing}

Each research question is written as a graph node with:

- a stable section anchor;
- a one-sentence definition or hypothesis;
- aliases when terminology can drift;
- links to internal docs, API surfaces, and roadmap gates;
- citations through `docs/references.bib` when a question depends on prior
  work.

New thesis docs and public docstrings should follow the same pattern so later
KG extraction can connect concepts, code, experiments, and evidence without
inferring relationships from prose alone.
